what_it_does: Defines BrandKit, a customer brand-kit settings screen for the platform kit. It renders a form: a main-logo dropzone, a company-name input that live-builds a slugified URL preview (https://conceptv.io/panotours/<slug>/projectname), an additional-logos dropzone, and a two-column panel pairing a colour-scheme list (seven labelled colour rows plus a 'Revert to Default' checkbox) against a live Preview card showing how a hub title/icons/description/button would look. The component holds the company name in state and is attached to window.
observations: ColourRow is a local component rendering a swatch + label
Seven colour rows with hardcoded hex values: #1F1A12, #FFFFFF, #D8B820, #FFFFFF, #988058, #A89678, #6B5F47 — labelled menu bg, menu text, button, button text, title, icon, description text
Company-name input is controlled (useState 'Acme Architecture'); URL preview slugifies via .toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,'-')
Preview card uses tokens (var(--bg-surface), --r-lg, --shadow-card, --accent-bronze) and emoji room icons (🛏 🛋 🛁 🚗)
Mixes token references (var(--r-sm), --border-faint, --font-body) with hardcoded inline literals (gap:14, minHeight:160, fontSize:13, rgba(31,26,18,.08))
cv-info-i 'i' badges next to several field labels; placeholder lorem-ipsum description text
Two dropzones rendered as cv-dropzone with an 'add' + glyph (no upload wiring shown)
standouts: The live URL-slug preview that reacts to the company name input as you type
The side-by-side colour-scheme editor ↔ Preview card showing the branding applied
conventions: Uses cv- class names and design tokens for the structural look, consistent with the design system
Aliases useState as useState_bk and attaches to window — same bundle-safe pattern as siblings
Breaks the no-raw-px/no-raw-hex rule heavily: colour rows pass literal hex, layout uses inline px gaps/heights
concerns: Seven hardcoded hex colours and an rgba literal bypass the token system the project mandates (though these may be sample brand data, not theme tokens)
Dropzones and Revert checkbox have no interactive wiring beyond display
Heavy inline-style px literals (gap, minHeight, fontSize) rather than tokens
notes: The brand-kit screen: logo dropzones + company-name-to-URL live preview + a seven-colour scheme editor mirrored by a live hub Preview card.
questions: Are the seven hex values sample customer brand data (legitimately literal) or should they reference tokens?
Is the colour scheme editable/persisted, or is this a static display of the rows?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — README calls it the 'customer brand-kit form' and that is what it renders.
apparent_themes: Branding/white-label customization of a Virtual Hub
Live preview of applied settings (URL slug, colour scheme)
intention_signals: Aims to let a customer define their hub's branding — logo, name (which drives the public URL), and a colour scheme — and immediately see it reflected in a representative preview before publishing.
novelty: The dual live previews: company name → public panotour URL slug, and colour scheme → a rendered hub card.
contribution: Contributes the brand-kit / white-label customization surface: the colour-scheme vocabulary (seven named hub colour roles), the name→URL slug preview, and the colour-editor↔preview pairing.
summary_for_embedding: React BrandKit screen: a customer branding form with logo dropzones, a company-name-driven URL slug preview, and a seven-colour scheme editor mirrored by a live hub preview card.
intention_for_embedding: To let a customer define and preview their Virtual Hub's branding — logo, name/URL, and colour scheme — before publishing.
